title: Drywall and Ceiling Tile Installers
description: Apply plasterboard or other wallboard to ceilings or interior walls of buildings. Apply or mount acoustical tiles or blocks, strips, or sheets of shock-absorbing materials to ceilings and walls of buildings to reduce or reflect sound. Materials may be of decorative quality. Includes lathers who fasten wooden, metal, or rockboard lath to walls, ceilings, or partitions of buildings to provide support base for plaster, fireproofing, or acoustical material.

tasks.text: Read blueprints or other specifications to determine methods of installation, work procedures, or material or tool requirements.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Measure and mark surfaces to lay out work, according to blueprints or drawings, using tape measures, straightedges or squares, and marking devices.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Fit and fasten wallboard or drywall into position on wood or metal frameworks, using glue, nails, or screws.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Measure and cut openings in panels or tiles for electrical outlets, windows, vents, plumbing, or other fixtures, using keyhole saws or other cutting tools.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Assemble or install metal framing or decorative trim for windows, doorways, or vents.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Cut metal or wood framing and trim to size, using cutting tools.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Inspect furrings, mechanical mountings, or masonry surfaces for plumbness and level, using spirit or water levels.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Cut fixture or border tiles to size, using keyhole saws, and insert them into surrounding frameworks.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Cut and screw together metal channels to make floor or ceiling frames, according to plans for the location of rooms or hallways.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Hang drywall panels on metal frameworks of walls and ceilings in offices, schools, or other large buildings, using lifts or hoists to adjust panel heights, when necessary.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Trim rough edges from wallboard to maintain even joints, using knives.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Suspend angle iron grids or channel irons from ceilings, using wire.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Coordinate work with drywall finishers who cover the seams between drywall panels.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Install horizontal and vertical metal or wooden studs to frames so that wallboard can be attached to interior walls.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Scribe and cut edges of tile to fit walls where wall molding is not specified.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Hang dry lines to wall moldings to guide positioning of main runners.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Fasten metal or rockboard lath to the structural framework of walls, ceilings, or partitions of buildings, using nails, screws, staples, or wire-ties.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Install blanket insulation between studs and tack plastic moisture barriers over insulation.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Seal joints between ceiling tiles and walls.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Remove existing plaster, drywall, or paneling, using crowbars and hammers.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Apply or mount acoustical tile or blocks, strips, or sheets of shock-absorbing materials to ceilings or walls of buildings to reduce reflection of sound or to decorate rooms.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Mount tile, using adhesives, or by nailing, screwing, stapling, or wire-tying lath directly to structural frameworks.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Nail channels or wood furring strips to surfaces to provide mounting for tile.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Install metal lath where plaster applications will be exposed to weather or water, or for curved or irregular surfaces.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Apply cement to backs of tiles and press tiles into place, aligning them with layout marks or joints of previously laid tile.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Wash concrete surfaces before mounting tile to increase adhesive qualities of surfaces, using washing soda and zinc sulfate solution.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.
